description_long: This is a classic pipe wrench with a robust, metallic body and a wooden handle. The wrench features a large, adjustable jaw with serrated teeth for gripping pipes or other cylindrical objects. The adjustment mechanism is visible as a knurled knob and sliding lower jaw, allowing the user to change the jaw opening width. The handle is cylindrical and tapers slightly towards the end, with a darker, wood-textured finish that contrasts with the blackened metal of the head and shaft. The overall construction appears solid and heavy-duty, suitable for plumbing or mechanical work. There are no visible brand markings or significant signs of wear, and the tool appears well-maintained.
description: A heavy-duty pipe wrench with a black metal adjustable jaw and a brown wooden handle, featuring a knurled adjustment knob and serrated gripping teeth.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the adjustable jaw, serrated teeth, and the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side profile view highlighting the thinness of the wrench and the alignment of the handle and jaw.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with the adjustment mechanism and jaw profile more visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the thinness and the handle's cylindrical shape.
attribute_handle_grip_pattern: smooth wooden handle with no visible grip pattern or texture
